🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 Iran’s Decision Not to Attend Negotiations Remains Unchanged So Far ▪️ According to information obtained by Tasnim’s reporter, the decision not to join negotiations remains unchanged despite Donald Trump claiming the U.S. team is heading to Pakistan. Iran participation depends on key preconditions being met, with the reported naval blockade issue seen as a major obstacle. ▪️ Iranian officials also cite further U.S. demands and say talks are pointless unless Washington changes its approach. ▪️ Until major obstacles are resolved, Iran says it will not take part in what it calls an American political show. 🚩 @RezistanceTrench

↩️ Reply to OSINT Open Source Intel Ending U.S. military aid to Israel sounds like a disaster. It’s not. Israel has been slowly weaning itself off U.S. aid. This isn’t a shock scenario. It’s already in motion. Here’s what some people miss. Israel is a small country with a manpower constraint. Always has been. But autonomy and robots are about to change that equation. And Israel has about 4,500 square miles of open desert to build at scale. Put those together and you get something big. A serious ramp in weapons production over the next decade. Israel is already around the 7th largest arms exporter in the world. For a country its size, that’s not normal. And it’s not staying there. Now the part people don’t like to say. U.S. aid isn’t charity. It’s alignment with benefits on both sides. A strong Israel needed for U.S. interests in the Middle East. But it also gives Washington leverage. The more self sufficient Israel becomes, the less that leverage matters. At that point, the relationship shifts. Not weaker. More balanced. True partners. Ending U.S military aid to Israel is a good thing. Rahm Emanuel: "No more U.S. military aid, financial assistance from the taxpayers for Israel. You're a country like all other allies of ours, Japan, South Korea, the Brits, the Germans. You're going to pay full price; you can buy what you want, but you have to abide by the laws — that should be it." "No more U.S. taxpayer support... I was in the room when President Obama's largest assistance was under President Obama. We did the funding for the Iron Dome. But here, the days of taxpayer subsidizing Israel are over." "No more financial aid." - Open Source Intel tweet

text: ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi and Pakistani Foreign Minister Dar today for the second day in a row after holding a call yesterday. they discussed regional developments and the ceasefire situation with Pakistan highlighting its ongoing efforts to end the war and establish regional peace, reaffirming readiness to continue consultations and mediation. Araghchi thanked Pakistan for its role in facilitating talks, while stressing Iran’s engagement in diplomacy to end the conflict. He also criticised recent U.S. actions, citing alleged ceasefire violations, threats to Iranian ports and vessels, and what he described as inconsistent and unreasonable demands. @wfwitness
text_zh: ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 伊朗外交部长阿拉格齐与巴基斯坦外交部长达尔继昨日通话后，今日连续第二天进行沟通。他们讨论了地区局势和停火情况，巴基斯坦强调其为结束战争和建立地区和平所做的持续努力，并重申愿意继续进行磋商和调解。阿拉格齐感谢巴基斯坦在促进会谈中发挥的作用，同时强调伊朗致力于通过外交手段结束冲突。他还批评了美国近期的行动，列举了所谓的停火违规行为、对伊朗港口和船只的威胁，以及他所描述的反复无常且不合理的要求。 @wfwitness
